action.reasoning: Observation: Best Z500 RMSE = 460.9 (iter 8, single model base_ch=128). Ensemble of 3 models in iter 10 got 465.6 (worse because individual models were ~468-469 each, likely due to buggy EMA from truncated train.py). Models consistently overfit at epoch 4-5. Analysis: Need to improve single model quality. Key changes: 1) Add time derivative (diff = t - t-6h) as extra input channels (in_channels=8), providing explicit trend info. 2) Clean implementation without buggy EMA. 3) Lower LR (1e-4) with proper warmup+cosine to slow overfitting. 4) Delete old model_s*.pt files so predict.py uses only the new best model. 5) Use 4-shift TTA (0,16,32,48) which worked well in iter 8. Time budget: ~46 min remaining, training ~20 min for 20 epochs with stride=1.
